channel.title: Proverbs on Christian Internet Resources
channel.description: Recent content in Proverbs on Christian Internet Resources

item.title: Proverbs 1
item.description:
Proverbs Chapter 1

Conversational Yahveh Covenant

1 Proverbs of Solomon Ben-David, King of Israel

                                                             2for knowing wisdom and instruction,

                                                 for understanding words that give insight,

                                                             3for receiving instruction in smart living,

                                                 goodness, justice, and equity;

                                                             4for giving prudence to the simple,

                                                  and knowledge and discretion to the young;

item.title: Proverbs 2
item.description:
Proverbs Chapter 2

1 Son, if you accept what I say

            and value my commandments,

      2 if you’re attentive to wisdom

            and inclined to understanding,

      3 if you cry out for insight

            and call out for comprehension,

      4 if you look for it like silver

item.title: Proverbs 3
item.description:
Proverbs Chapter 3

1 Son, don’t forget what I’ve taught you;  

      take my commandments to heart;

         2they’ll add peace and length to your life.

3 Don’t let kindness and truth slip away from you;

            tie them around your neck;

            write them on your heart.

         4That way you’ll find blessing

item.title: Proverbs 4
item.description:
Proverbs Chapter 4

1 Listen to my instruction, children;

      pay attention and learn.

2 I’ll give you helpful guidance;

      don’t abandon it.

3 When I was my father’s son,

      my mother’s tender only boy,

      4 he taught me,

            “Hold fast to what I say;

item.title: Proverbs 5
item.description:
Proverbs Chapter 5

1 Pay attention to my wisdom, son;

                  listen to what I understand

                  2so you can use discretion

                  and express knowledge.

         3An immoral woman’s lips drip honey;

                  her speech is more soothing than oil;

                  4but in the end she’s as bitter as wormwood,

item.title: Proverbs 6
item.description:
Proverbs Chapter 6

1 Son, if you sign for your neighbor

            or give a pledge for a stranger,

      2 if you get snared by a promise you’ve made

            or caught by what you’ve said,

      3 do this to deliver yourself:

            since you’re at his disposal,

item.title: Proverbs 7
item.description:
Proverbs Chapter 7

1 Take my advice, son;

      treasure up my commandments inside you.

2 Keep my commands so you can have a life;

      guard my teachings like the pupil in your eye.

3 Tie them on your fingers;

      write them on your heart.       

4 Call wisdom your sister and insight your friend,

item.title: Proverbs 8
item.description:
Proverbs Chapter 8

1 Wisdom calls out,

      and intelligence raises its voice.

2 It takes its stand on high places,

      where footpaths meet.

3 It shouts at the city gates,

         4“I’m calling out to you;

            my voice is aimed at everyone.

         5Learn prudence, simple ones;

item.title: Proverbs 9
item.description:
Proverbs Chapter 9

The ones that hate me love death.”                            Prov 8:32-36

1 Wisdom has built her house;

      She’s carved its seven columns.

2 She’s done her butchering, mixed her wine,

      and set her table.

3 She’s sent her maids

      to call out from the heights of the city:

                  4“Whoever is naïve, turn in here!”

item.title: Proverbs 10
item.description:
Proverbs Chapter 10

1 Proverbs of Solomon

A wise son makes a happy father,

but a foolish one grieves his mother.

                  2Ill-gotten gain doesn’t gain you anything,

            but goodness delivers you from death.

3 Yahveh won’t let good people go hungry;

he thwarts what the wicked want.

                  4A lazy hand makes for poverty;

item.title: Proverbs 11
item.description:
Proverbs Chapter 11

1 Yahveh detests a false balance;

he delights in accurate weights.

                  2Dishonor follows pride;

            but wisdom lies with the humble.

3 The integrity of the wise gives them direction;

the dishonesty of the treacherous destroys them.

                  4Wealth won’t help you when judgment comes,

item.title: Proverbs 12
item.description:
Proverbs Chapter 12

1 Whoever loves discipline loves knowledge;

whoever hates to be corrected stays ignorant.

                  2Yahveh gives grace to the good,

            but condemns those who concoct evil.

3 Sinfulness brings instability,

but godliness grows deep roots.

                  4A good wife is her husband’s crown,

            but one that embarrasses him is like cancer in his bones.

item.title: Proverbs 13
item.description:
Proverbs Chapter 13

1 Wise children accept their father’s discipline,

but scoffers don’t pay attention to correction.

                  2From the influence of your words,

                  people respond willingly;

            but treacherous people want to force things through.

3 Be careful what you say, and you’ll save yourself;

talk too much, and you’ll do yourself in.

item.title: Proverbs 14
item.description:
Proverbs Chapter 14

1 A wise woman builds up her home;

a foolish one tears it down with her own hands.

                  2We show respect for Yahveh by pursuing godliness;

            being crooked in our ways means we don’t respect him.

3 The tongues of fools become a rod for their back;

the lips of the wise save them.

item.title: Proverbs 15
item.description:
Proverbs Chapter 15

1 A gentle answer deflects anger;

harsh words make tempers flare.

                  2The wise dispense knowledge;

            fools expound foolishness.

3 Yahveh sees everything;

he watches the evil and the good.

                  4Gentle words are a tree of life,

            but perverse talk breaks people’s spirit.

item.title: Proverbs 16
item.description:
Proverbs Chapter 16

1 We have intentions,

but Yahveh provides the proper course of action.

                  2Our ways may seem pure to us,

            but Yahveh weighs our motives.

3 Commit your work to Yahveh,

and you’ll succeed.

                  4Yahveh has a purpose for everything,

            even for sinners in troubled times.

item.title: Proverbs 17
item.description:
Proverbs Chapter 17

1 A dry morsel eaten in peace tastes better

than a feast in a house full of conflict.

                  2A wise servant will rule a shameful son

            and share in the inheritance among brothers.

3 Fire refines silver and a furnace purifies gold,

but Yahveh assays the heart.

item.title: Proverbs 18
item.description:
Proverbs Chapter 18

Prov 17:25-28

1 Unfriendly people pursue selfish ends;

and don’t use common sense.

                  2Fools don’t delight in learning,

            only in telling what they think.

3 When sinners come, contempt comes with them;

and with dishonor comes disgrace.

                  4Wise thoughts are deep water;

            wisdom is the spring for a babbling brook.

item.title: Proverbs 19
item.description:
Proverbs Chapter 19

1 The honest poor are better off

than dishonest fools.

                  2It’s not good to not know;

            enthusiasm doesn’t compensate for ignorance.

3 When people’s foolishness brings them to ruin,      

they blame Yahveh.

                  4Wealth draws lots of friends

            that desert you if poverty comes.

item.title: Proverbs 20
item.description:
Proverbs Chapter 20

1 Wine is a mocker, beer is a brawler;

whoever errs in them is not wise.

                  2A king’s anger is like a lion’s growl;

            stirring it up forfeits your life.

3 Staying away from conflict honors you;

any fool can fight.

                  4Anyone too lazy to plow

item.title: Proverbs 21
item.description:
Proverbs Chapter 21

1 A king’s heart in Yahveh’s hand

is like a stream he can direct where he wants.

                  2Your own way seems right to you,

            but Yahveh weighs your heart.

3 Yahveh wants goodness and justice

more than sacrifice.

                  4Arrogant eyes and a proud heart

item.title: Proverbs 22
item.description:
Proverbs Chapter 22

1 A good name means more than great wealth;

being blessed is better than having silver and gold.

                  2The rich and the poor have this in common:

            Yahveh made them both.

3 Prudent people see evil and avoid it;

but the simpleminded rush on ahead and suffer for it.

item.title: Proverbs 23
item.description:
Proverbs Chapter 23

They’ll serve kings, not just ordinary people.            Prov 22:24-29

1 When you’re dining with a ruler,

            consider carefully what he sets in front of you.

      2 Put a knife to your throat

            if you have a big appetite.

         3Don’t crave very many of his delicacies;

item.title: Proverbs 24
item.description:
Proverbs Chapter 24

I’ll want another drink.”                                Prov 23:29-35

1 Don’t envy bad people

            or want to be around them;

         2their minds devise violence;

            their lips stir up trouble.

3 Wisdom builds a house;

      intelligence establishes a home;

         4knowledge fills its rooms with precious, pleasant wealth.

item.title: Proverbs 25
item.description:
Proverbs Chapter 25

1

More of Solomon’s Proverbs That the Men of Hezekiah,

King of Judah, Transcribed

2 It’s God’s privilege not to reveal things

it’s a king’s responsibility to figure them out.

                  3A king’s mind is as unsearchable

            as the sky is high and the earth is deep.

4 Remove the dross from silver,

item.title: Proverbs 26
item.description:
Proverbs Chapter 26

1 Like snow in summer and rain at harvest,

honor is out of place on a fool.

                  2Like a flitting sparrow, like a fluttering swallow,

            an undeserved curse doesn’t land on its intended victim.

3 A whip is for a horse,

a bridle is for a donkey,

item.title: Proverbs 27
item.description:
Proverbs Chapter 27

1 Don’t brag about tomorrow;

you don’t know what’s going to happen.

                  2Let somebody else praise you, not your own mouth;

            a stranger, not your own lips.

3 A rock is heavy and sand weighs a lot,

but a fool’s resentment outweighs either one.

                  4Anger and wrath are bad enough,

item.title: Proverbs 28
item.description:
Proverbs Chapter 28

1 Sinners run when no one’s chasing,

but the good are as bold as lions.

                  2When a country sins, lots of leaders vie for control;

            but wise and knowledgeable leaders bring stability.

3 The poor that oppress the poor

are like a driving rain that destroys crops.

                  4Those who leave the Law commend the sinful,

item.title: Proverbs 29
item.description:
Proverbs Chapter 29

1 If you stiffen your neck after repeated correction,         

you’ll suddenly be broken beyond recovery.

                  2When good people rule, people are happy;

            when corrupt leaders take over, the citizens groan.

3 A son that loves wisdom makes his father happy,

but one that visits prostitutes squanders his wealth.

item.title: Proverbs 30
item.description:
Proverbs Chapter 30

1 The Words of Agur Ben-Jakeh, an Oracle

Agur says to Ithiel and Ucal:

2 Surely I’m too stupid to be human;

      I don’t have common sense.

3 I haven’t mastered wisdom; 

      I don’t know the Holy One.

4 Who but God goes up to the sky and back down

item.title: Proverbs 31
item.description:
Proverbs Chapter 31

3 don’t waste your strength

      on the kind of women that destroy kings.

4 Lemuel, it’s not for kings to guzzle wine

            or for rulers to get addicted to alcohol;

         5the fear is that they’ll forget the Law

            and not give justice to the oppressed.
